description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, commonly seen in early 20th-century urban environments. The main body is constructed from polished dark wood, featuring a rectangular box with a slanted lower shelf and ornate brass hardware. Two large metallic bells are mounted on the upper front, above a black rotary dial and a brass nameplate. The left side holds a black earpiece attached by a straight cord, while the right side features a wooden crank handle for manual operation. The back and sides are plain wood, with the rear showing a mounting plate and the right side displaying the crank mechanism. The overall design is robust and decorative, reflecting historical craftsmanship and utility.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden body, brass hardware, and a black earpiece attached by a cord. Features include dual bells, a rotary dial, and a side crank handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual metallic bells, rotary dial, brass nameplate, and the black earpiece resting on the left side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wooden crank handle and the depth of the wooden box, with the earpiece partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back and the mounting plate, with minimal hardware visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the black earpiece hanging on a brass hook and the side profile of the crank handle.
